get.description: Retrieve tags and associated phone numbers. You can filter by phone number, tag name, or tag value. If you filter by phone number, only tags associated with the given phone numbers will be returned.

patch.description: Update the phone numbers associated with a tag. You can either move phone numbers to a specific tag or remove phone numbers from the tag. A MOVE operation will create a tag if it does not exist. If a phone number is already associated with a tag name, a MOVE operation will replace the tag value.

delete.description: Delete a phone number tag and dissociate all phone numbers from that tag.

get.description: Retrieves the moveTns orders for the given account ID. A maximum of 1,000 orders can be retrieved per request. If no date range or specific query parameter is provided, the order results will be limited to the last two years

post.description:
Creates a MoveTnsOrder request to move numbers to the given site/sub-account ID and SIP peer/location ID as specified in the body. A successfully submitted order will have a status of "RECEIVED". A successfully completed order will have a status of "COMPLETE" if all of the telephone numbers were successfully moved and "PARTIAL" if some of the telephone numbers were moved. A failed order will have a status of "FAILED" and no telephone numbers would have been moved.

Numbers can be moved to sub-accounts (or sites) and locations (or SIP peers) of a different account (if the user has access to both accounts and the proper roles). This can be done by specifying the SourceAccountId parameter. The user can also specify how to handle E911 service on the number (if it exists) using the optional e911ServiceAction parameter

get.description: Retrieve information about a MoveTnsOrder with specified ID
get.summary: Fetch Move Telephone Numbers order status

get.description: Retrieves the history of the specified MoveTnsOrder
get.summary: Fetch Move Telephone Numbers order history

post.description:
This API provides a dry-run capability for MoveTn orders that enables users to obtain the validation information without actually executing the order.

This is a synchronous process. Instead of generating an order this will provide a response that includes validation information for the submitted MoveTn request. This will include numbers that can be successfully moved and numbers where there is some compatibility or validation issue.

This endpoint creates a bulk MoveTNs order which will generate a number of child MoveTNs orders and track their
progress. The amount and details of the child orders will be determined based on the information provided in the
`orderDetails` element. Each item there should include a unique combination of details based on the desired
destination and configuration of the TNs after the move.

get.description:
This endpoint returns the state of the bulk order, along with summary information about the child
orders created from the bulk order submission. The response includes varying amounts of information based
on the status of the bulk order. The `verbose` query param can be specified to include the original details
which were supplied for the bulk move along with the child orders created for each set of details.
get.summary: Get Move TNs Bulk Order Status

get.description: List groups for a specific account

post.description: Create a new group to which phone numbers can be assigned for Number Reputation Management.

get.description: Get group information for a specific account and group ID

delete.description: Deletes a group by its ID for the specified account.

post.description: Update the assignment of multiple numbers to number reputation groups. This allows you to add numbers to a group by ID, or remove up to 5000 numbers from any number of groups. If one or more numbers is unable to be updated, the valid numbers will still be updated and returned in the response.

get.description: Get historical reputation status data for a specific phone number managed by Number Reputation Management. Returns a list of historical status entries sorted in reverse chronological order by scan time.
Get a list of customers for the specified account.
Get a specific customer for the specified account.

post.summary: Checks service activation status for a list of phone numbers
post.description: Accepts a list of phone numbers and returns the service activation status for each phone number. If service is not able to be activated, missing requirements will also be returned.

get.summary: Gets a list of service activation orders
get.description: Gets a list of the service activation orders belonging to the specified account. Orders can be filtered by customer order ID (exact match or starts with). Results are paginated.

post.description: Creates a service activation order to activate service on a list of telephone numbers.

get.description: Gets a service activation order with the specified orderId.

Basic.description: Basic authentication is a simple authentication scheme built into the HTTP protocol. To use it, send your HTTP requests with an Authorization header that contains the word Basic followed by a space and a base64-encoded string `username:password`. Example: `Authorization: Basic dXNlcm5hbWU6cGFzc3dvcmQ=`

Bearer.description:
Bearer authentication (also called token authentication) is an HTTP authentication scheme that involves security tokens called bearer tokens. The name “Bearer authentication” can be understood as “give access to the bearer of this token.” The client must send this token in the Authorization header when making requests to protected resources.

Example: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

getPhoneNumbersTagsResponse.title: List phone number tags and associated phone numbers

phoneNumberTagActions.description: The MOVE action moves phone numbers to a specific tag. The REMOVE action removes phone numbers from a specific tag.

tagName.description: Name of tag associated with a phone number.

tagValue.description: Value of tag associated with a phone number.

rel.description: Specifies the relationship between this link and the resource.

subAccountId.description: The ID of the sub-account to which the phone numbers will be moved.

locationId.description: The ID of the location to which the phone numbers will be moved.

phoneNumbers.description: The list of phone numbers in E.164 format.

e911ServiceAction.description:
This parameter provides flexibility on handling the E911 service of a number when moving to a new account.
The default is to “MOVE,” but that may not be possible, resulting in an error where DELETE is required.
For example, if moving numbers to an account that uses PIDFLO for E911 then E911ServiceAction = DELETE
is required, and the E911 service will need to be configured on the numbers after the move.

messagingService.description:
The action to be taken on the messaging service when moving numbers. This field
ensures that the destination account has compatible messaging settings to support moving numbers. If the 
validation passes, the numbers are moved without altering their existing messaging settings. However, 
if the validation fails, the order will be rejected with an error messaging indicating incompatible settings.

If set to `useDestinationSettings`, this will apply the destination location's messaging settings to the number.

If set to `useCurrentNumberSettings`, this will validate and apply whether the destination location 
can accept the numbers' existing messaging settings.

voiceConfigurationPackageId.title: Voice Configuration Package ID
voiceConfigurationPackageId.description:
The identifier of the voice configuration package containing the voice origination settings. Upon activation, 
telephone numbers will be configured with the voice origination settings specified in the voice configuration
package. This is required if the numbers currently have voice service and are being moved to another account
which has the voice configuration package feature.

requirementsPackageId.title: Requirements Package ID
requirementsPackageId.description:
The identifier of the requirements package containing the end user details for the telephone numbers. This
is required if the numbers are being moved across accounts and if they are a part of a country and number type
which has end user validation requirements. The provided requirements package must be approved and compatible
with the telephone numbers being moved.

bypassMessagingLossBlock.description:
A flag that determines whether to bypass the messaging loss block. 

If set to **true**, no error will occur when moving a number with messaging to a location or account that does
not support messaging; messaging will simply be disabled in this case.

If set to **false**, an error will be triggered if such a move is attempted, preventing the move from processing.

Default value is false.

bypassMessagingCampaignBlock.description:
A flag that determines whether to bypass the messaging campaign block.

If set to **true** and the numbers are associated with a messaging campaign, the move TN request will succeed,
but the campaign will be removed from the numbers during the move.

If set to **false** and the numbers are associated with a messaging campaign, the move request will 
fail with an error.

Default value is false.

sourceAccountId.description: ID of source account, in the case of moving numbers across accounts.

migrateCampaign.description:
Flag for cross-account moves denoting whether any campaigns associated with phone numbers from the order should
also be migrated to the new account. If set to `true`, all phone numbers from each involved campaign must be
moved in the same bulk order and the messaging settings of the source and destination accounts must be the same.
If set to `false`, the moved numbers will be removed from their current campaigns if they are moved to a new account.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date/time when the order was last modified.

orderDate.description: The date/time when the order was created.

orderIds.description:
A list of order IDs for child orders generated from this group of phone number configurations. If there were more than 5000
phone numbers for one grouping of bulk order details, multiple child orders will be created.

orderId.description: Unique identifier for the order that was created.

userId.description: The ID of the user who created the order.

bulkMoveTnsOrderStatus.description:
Processing status of the bulk Move TNs order.

RECEIVED - Order has been received without any synchronous errors and will be processed through further validation.

PROCESSING - Order has passed validation and is being processed asynchronously. Some child orders may have completed, but at least one is in progress.

FAILED - This implies that the order did not pass synchronous validation (Either number specific or non-number specific errors will be displayed).

COMPLETE - All child orders have been processed and the order is complete. Review the statuses of each child order for details on the outcome.

childMoveTnsOrderStatus.description:
Processing status of the child Move TNs order.

RECEIVED - Order has been received without any synchronous errors and will be processed through further validation.

PROCESSING - The order has passed initial validation and is now being processed. This child order will now go through further asynchronous validation.

COMPLETE - This implies that this child order has been successfully processed and all numbers have been moved with the desired configurations.

FAILED - This implies that some asynchronous errors are associated with the child order. No numbers were moved within this specific child order.

PARTIAL - This implies that some numbers were successfully moved while others encountered asynchronous errors within this specific child order.

bulkMoveTnsErrors.description:
Errors which arose on the bulk order during validation. Any phone numbers which encounter errors at this point will not
be moved and will remain on their current account and location.

code.description: Error code unique to the specific failure which occurred.

phoneNumbers.description: List of phone numbers for which the error occurred.

actionEnum.description: The action to take with the specified phone numbers.

groupId.description: The ID of the group to which phone numbers are being added. Required for the `ADD` action and ignored for the `REMOVE` action.

action.description: The action that was taken on the phone numbers.

groupId.description: The ID of the group to which numbers are being added. Returned only for the `ADD` action.

updatedPhoneNumbers.description: The list of phone numbers that were successfully updated by the request, in E.164 format.

description.description: Details about the issue which may require user attention.

TelephoneNumbers.description: List of telephone numbers to be moved. Maximum of 5,000 allowed per POST

TelephoneNumber.description: Ten digit phone number with no dots or dashes

SiteId.description: A site id to which telephone number will be moved

CustomerOrderId.description: Internal customer order id for tracking the order. Only alphanumeric values, dashes and spaces are allowed. Max length is 255 characters

SipPeerId.description: A sippeer id of provided site to which telephone number will be moved

SourceAccountId.description: Id of source account, in the case of moving numbers across accounts

E911ServiceAction.description: Only valid when moving to a different account using SourceAccountId. When set to move (default) the E911 service information (if applied to the number) will be moved with the number to the new account. When set to delete the E911 service will be removed from the number

orderDate.description: This is the date/time when the order history entry was created.

note.description: This is the order note that accompanied the order history entry.

author.description: This is the user that initiated the event that caused the order history to be updated.

customerOrderId.description: Internal customer order id for tracking the order. Only alphanumeric values, dashes and spaces are allowed. Max length is 255 characters

locationId.description: A locationId id of provided subAccount to which telephone number will be moved

childOrders.description:

The full details for each child order can be retrieved from the [Fetch MoveTNs Order Status API](#tag/Move-TNs/operation/readMoveTnsOrder).

groupIntentEnum.title: Group Intent
groupIntentEnum.description:
The intent or purpose of the group. This indicates how the phone numbers in the group will be used.
- GOVN: Government
- SATE: Sales/Telemarketing
- ACCS: Account Services
- SURV: Survey
- POLI: Political
- FUND: Fundraising
- DEBC: Debt Collection
- APPR: Appointment Reminder
- INFO: Information
- CUSE: Customer Services
- APPS: Appointment Setting
- BILL: Billing
- EMER: Emergency
- ATTO: Attorney/Law
- FSER: Financial Service
- FRES: First Responder
- HOSP: Health Care/Hospital
- INSU: Insurance
- PHAR: Pharmacy
- REST: Real Estate
- COLL: School/College
- NPF: Non-Profit
- MUL: Multi use line

      <Author>admin</Author>
      <Status>complete</Status>

</OrderHistoryWrapper>

tags.name: Move TNs
tags.description: Manage orders for moving telephone numbers across sub-accounts and locations.

tags.name: Move TNs Bulk Orders
tags.description:
Manage bulk orders for moving telephone numbers across sub-accounts and locations.

This API is currently in Early Access and is only available to some accounts. Please contact your account manager for more information on obtaining access.

tags.name: Move TNs Checker
tags.description: Check the validity of telephone numbers for moving across sub-accounts and locations.

tags.name: Number Reputation Management
tags.description: Manage phone number reputation, including grouping phone numbers and checking their status.

tags.name: Phone Number Tagging
tags.description: Add custom tags to non-hosted phone numbers.

tags.name: Service Activation
tags.description: Manage service activation of voice features like inbound, outbound national, and outbound international.
